About: It is about a short-distance running race.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with the Stawell Athletic Club.
It is associated with the Victorian Athletic League.
It is associated with professional running.
It is associated with the Victorian gold rush.
BelongsTo: It belongs to the Grampian Mountains district of western Victoria.
CausedBy: It is caused by the COVID-19 pandemic in Australia.
ConsistsOf: It consists of events for men and women of all ages and abilities.
It consists of races across distances from 70 to 3,200 metres.
It consists of seven races, most run in multiple heats.
CreatedAround: It was created around the end of the Victorian gold rush.
HasA: It has a 120 m grass track.
It has lane ropes rather than painted lines.
It has an extensive bookmaker's compound.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by being Australia's oldest and richest short-distance running race.
It is characterized by competitors being handicapped according to their form.
It is characterized by a maximum handicap of 10 metres.
It is characterized by the idea that all runners should theoretically cross the line at the same time.
It is characterized by the handicapping system pitting local runners against international professionals.
It is characterized by gambling being allowed in the venue.
It is characterized by the Women's Gift having equal prizemoney with the men's since 2015.
It is characterized by the final being run on grass over 120 metres up a slight gradient.

It was held in Ballarat City Oval.
HeldOn: It was held on Easter weekend.
It was held on the holiday Monday.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by previous form.
It was influenced by the need to perform slightly below best in lead-up events.
It was influenced by the COVID-19 pandemic in Australia.
IsA: It is Australia's oldest and richest short-distance running race.
It is the main event in an annual carnival.
It is the most prestigious footrace in Australia.
KnownAs: It is known as the "Easter Gift".
KnownFor: It is known for being Australia's oldest and richest short-distance running race.
It is known for its handicap system.
It is known for the Eric Cumming Memorial trophy.
It is known for attracting sponsors.
It is known for its financial difficulties.
LocatedIn: It is located in the Grampian Mountains district of western Victoria.

It is a member of the Victorian Athletic League.
OccurredAt: It occurred at Central Park, Stawell.
OccurredDuring: It occurred during the Easter weekend.

It occurred during the COVID-19 pandemic.
OccurredAround: It occurred around the end of the Victorian gold rush.

It is part of a number of entertainments put on by the townspeople over Easter.
PublishedAs: It was published as the "Powercor Stawell Gift".
UsedAs: It is used as a handicap system.
It is used as a basis for determining winners.
It is used as a measure of runner's form.
